title: Doc-Init Rec#1 — fab init forensic.json + detector extension
description: Extend packages/cli/src/scanner/detector.ts to return {kind, version, subkind} for Cocos Creator 2.x/3.x distinction and Vite/Next version detection. Add packages/cli/src/scanner/forensic.ts implementing buildForensicReport(target) that produces a ForensicReport conforming to the @fabric/shared ForensicReport schema. Extend init.ts initFabric() to write forensic.json after the existing 3 writeNewFile calls, install SKILL.md and hook (handled in TASK-003/004), and emit stdout reason text triggering the agents-md-init skill.

files.target: FrameworkInfo return type + Cocos 2.x/3.x detection logic
files.change: Extend FrameworkInfo to { kind, version, subkind, evidence[] }; read project.config.json creator.version for Cocos Creator version; distinguish 2.x (subkind:'javascript-traditional') vs 3.x (subkind:'typescript-component'); extract Vite/Next version from package.json dependencies

New file: import detectFramework from detector.ts; scan topology (total_files, by_ext, key_dirs, max_depth); identify entry_points (top-level .ts/.js in key script dirs); Smart Sampling — read first 30 lines of each entry_point with pattern_hint; build recommendations_for_skill array based on framework.kind; return ForensicReport conforming to @fabric/shared schema

files.target: initFabric() function — append forensic.json write + stdout reason at lines 75-106
files.change: After existing writeNewFile x3 (AGENTS.md/agents.meta.json/human-lock.json), add: (1) call buildForensicReport(target) and writeNewFile(forensic.json, JSON.stringify(report, null, 2)); (2) non-destructive guard: if forensic.json already exists, ABORT with consistent message; (3) stdout: console.log reason text ending with 'use the agents-md-init skill'

implementation: Update packages/cli/src/scanner/detector.ts: add version and subkind to FrameworkInfo type; implement readCreatorVersion(target) to parse project.config.json; add Cocos 2.x vs 3.x subkind branching logic; add vite/next version extraction from target package.json dependencies

Create packages/cli/src/scanner/forensic.ts: define buildForensicReport(target) function; compute topology by walking target directory with fs.readdirSync; collect entry_points from key dirs; implement Smart Sampling (readFileSync + slice to 30 lines); assemble recommendations_for_skill from framework.kind switch; import ForensicReport type from @fabric/shared

Update packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts: import buildForensicReport; after writeNewFile x3, add writeNewFile guard for forensic.json; call buildForensicReport and write JSON; append console.log with reason text including skill name
Verify non-destructive behavior: running fab init a second time should ABORT (forensic.json already exists)

reference.pattern: Extension of existing detector + new forensic scanner
reference.examples: Mirror non-destructive guard pattern from existing init.ts writeNewFile checks; mirror detector.ts detectFramework() shape for extension; ForensicReport schema from TASK-001

criteria: fab init on examples/werewolf-minigame-stub produces .fabric/forensic.json with framework.kind='cocos-creator', framework.version='3.8.0', framework.subkind='typescript-component'
forensic.json entry_points[] contains at least assets/scripts/Game.ts and assets/scripts/Player.ts
fab init stdout includes text containing 'agents-md-init' skill reference
Running fab init a second time on same target ABORTs with a clear message (non-destructive guard)
pnpm -r build passes with updated detector.ts FrameworkInfo type
ForensicReport produced by buildForensicReport passes @fabric/shared forensicReportSchema.safeParse() without errors

rationale.chosen_approach: Separate forensic.ts module keeps scanner concerns isolated from init.ts orchestration; detector.ts extension is additive (no breaking changes to existing detectors)
alternatives_considered: Inline forensic logic inside scan.ts (couples scan + init outputs)
FAB CLI calling Claude API directly for Layer 2 (rejected: dependency on AI at CLI level)
decision_factors: <200ms performance constraint (only file reads, no AST)
ForensicReport as inter-layer contract uses @fabric/shared schema from TASK-001
rationale.tradeoffs: Smart Sampling is line-truncation not AST — loses some semantic richness but stays fast and dependency-free

manual_checks: cd /path/to/werewolf-minigame-stub && fab init — verify .fabric/forensic.json is created with correct framework fields
fab init a second time — verify ABORT message about forensic.json already existing
Inspect forensic.json code_samples[] — each entry should have path, lines, snippet (≤30 lines), pattern_hint
success_metrics: forensic.json schema validation passes (ForensicReport zod.safeParse = success)
fab init completes in <200ms for werewolf-minigame-stub
unit: init-forensic.test.ts (created in TASK-010): initFabric on werewolf-minigame-stub asserts forensic.json fields

risks.description: project.config.json absent in Cocos 3.x projects or has different schema
risks.mitigation: Add try/catch around project.config.json parsing; fallback to version='unknown' and subkind inferred from tsconfig presence
risks.fallback: If version undetectable, forensic.json still valid — version field becomes 'unknown' and recommendations_for_skill adds a note
